prompt: I have a class `EmailValidator` with a single method `validate(email: String): Boolean` that runs a regex check. It's used in one place — the user registration handler. A teammate says I should introduce an `IEmailValidator` interface 'to follow SOLID / DIP'. Should I? Justify by SOLID, not by 'it depends'.

assertions.text: Recommends NOT introducing the interface (or strongly leans against it)
Articulates a criterion for when an interface earns its place: crosses an architectural boundary OR concept is volatile
Names this as anemic abstraction / unnecessary indirection (or equivalent — points out adding interface gives no decoupling benefit here)
Rejects 'there might be another implementation someday' as a sufficient justification
Acceptable revisit trigger: a real (non-speculative) second implementation requirement
